title: malva hispanica
intro: Malva hispanica, also known as marshmallow plant, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its large, ovate leaves and small, pink or white flowers. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, soothing, and protective properties, which can help to calm digestive issues, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. The medicinal properties of Malva hispanica have been attributed to its high content of mucilages, which can help to form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation. Herbal preparations of Malva hispanica can be made into teas, infusions, capsules, or topical creams, and have been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory conditions.
